name: Multi-Scene Product Launch
description: 27-scene branded product launch / commercial recreation in 9:16 — two identity-locked AI models (e.g. pink-haired girl + dreadlocked guy) interacting with a hero hardware product across BTS-commercial-set vignettes (treadmill, printed-fabric backdrop, lab-coat crew, foam-panel sound room). Built for full-spec product reveals where the product is the headline and identity drift across many short clips is the dominant risk. Master-shot pipeline: dedicated character + wardrobe + product hero refs are generated once and then passed as --ref on every keyframe and every i2v call to lock identity, wardrobe, hair, and product 1:1 across 20+ shots. Per-scene framing inherits the source shot via a locked storyboard ref. Audio stays source-1:1 (no VO regen) — for branded recreations the brand audio IS the point.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — the ONLY model that respects 9:16 (returns 768x1376 native, lanczos-upscale to 1080x1920). 4 variants per scene in parallel, $0.15/img. Each call passes 3-4 refs: locked storyboard frame from source + character base + wardrobe-reference + product hero. AVOID openai/gpt-5.4-image-2 for 9:16 — it ignores aspect-ratio and returns 1024x1024 squares. Use gpt-5.4-image-2 only for square product hero shots.
stackSummary.productMasters: openai/gpt-5.4-image-2 OR google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview — generate 5-8 product hero angles (three-quarter, side, front, flat-lay, macro, back tech-detail, floating tilt, low-angle stand) ONCE at project start. These become the product --ref pool for every scene gen.
stackSummary.characterMasters: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview — generate one identity-locked editorial portrait per character (pass the user-supplied base photo as ref, ask for tech-commercial restyle). Pick the cleanest variant for {{model_a}}-wardrobe-reference.png after pilot scene.
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter — 3-6s clips at $0.14/s, 720p (Remotion upscales). AVOID bytedance/seedance-2.0 — content-moderation filter blocks ~80% of photoreal AI faces with InputImageSensitiveContentDetected. Every clip needs STATIC LOCKED-OFF CAMERA + CONTINUOUS MID-MOTION blocks to fight Kling's deep-breath idle tic and ramp-up start tic.
stackSummary.voiceover: None — source audio is preserved 1:1 from the original mp4. For recreations the brand audio is the point. ElevenLabs only if user explicitly wants a translated VO replacement.
stackSummary.captions: None by default. Title cards rendered as Remotion <TitleCard /> components (brand wordmark, slogan, URL). For multi-state endcards use a <TitleCardCycle /> that swaps strings every (duration / N states).
stackSummary.music: None — source audio carries it. For non-recreation variants of this template, ElevenLabs Music with category-matched cue (cinematic for tech, electronic for sport, ambient for luxury).

constants.structure: title card (1s) -> 20-25 BTS commercial vignettes (1.5-5s each) -> 2-3 closing title cards (slogan, brand wordmark, URL)
constants.lighting: soft top key + cool fill, occasional rim — clean studio, no chiaroscuro
constants.cameraMotion: STATIC by default — only allow zoom / push-in if confirmed by source review
constants.minClipDuration: 3s (Kling minimum) — trim in Remotion via <Sequence durationInFrames>

variationAxes: product category (headphones, smartwatch, smart speaker, fitness tracker, camera, phone, gaming controller, drone)
set vignettes (treadmill BTS, printed backdrop, foam-panel sound room, neutral studio, city backdrop, moon backdrop, lab counter, dance floor)
product motion (static reveal, hands rotating dial, hands adjusting earcup, hands tracing edge, plug-in, on-off light cycle)
wardrobe direction (athletic / lab-coat / tech-couture / streetwear / minimal)
audio strategy (source 1:1 for recreation, ElevenLabs Music for original, source + brand jingle)
doNotCopyLiterally: Specific brand (Nothing), product (HP1), character identities (pink-hair girl / dreadlocks guy), olive-green / white-coat wardrobe choices. These are the {{slots}} — the durable template is the SHAPE (identity-lock pipeline + CAMERA/COMPOSITION/MID-MOTION blocks + multi-ref --ref chain + BTS commercial vignette grammar).
referenceNotes: User MUST supply: (1) a product reference photo (or pack of multi-angle product shots, ideally 4+), (2) one base photo per cast member (identity ref). Without these the template degrades to text-only descriptions which drift catastrophically across 27 scenes — that is the failure mode this template's master-shot pipeline exists to prevent. If recreating an existing commercial, also supply the source mp4 URL — run `ralphy ref pull` + `ralphy ref analyze-video --shots <N>` to extract the shot timeline (`scenes-<N>.json`) as the single source of truth.

slots.defaultExample: silver/white over-ear, transparent acrylic outer plate, two grey driver-vents, tiny red square accent, white padded headband
young woman, pale soft features, short shaggy pink hair
olive green vest over white long-sleeve crewneck, dark trousers
young man, deep skin tone, defined cheekbones, short twisted dreadlocks
printed pine-forest fabric banner (visible fabric texture, NOT a real forest)
printed moon-surface backdrop, foam-panel sound room, city-skyline banner

character-master-a.note: Sample identity-locked base portrait from the source project ({{model_a}}). Replace with the user's own {{model_a}} base photo before generating.

character-wardrobe-a.note: Sample wardrobe-locked frame for {{model_a}} — picked from the cleanest pilot. Establish your own equivalent after the first scene pilot.

character-master-b.note: Sample identity-locked base portrait from the source project ({{model_b}}). Replace with the user's own {{model_b}} base photo before generating.

character-wardrobe-b.note: Sample wardrobe-locked frame for {{model_b}} — picked from the cleanest pilot.

product-ref-01.note: Example product reference angle (three-quarter). The user MUST replace with their own product reference set.

costBallpark.notes: Source project (nothing-hp1-001, 27 scenes) spent ~$51 total with 6 prompt-iteration passes ($28 gemini-3-pro images, $14 Kling i2v, $8 gpt-5.4 product shots). A disciplined single-pass run using this template's locked prompt blocks should land at ~$20-25.

postmortemNote: The source project has no structured 6-file postmortem/ directory, but WORKFLOW.md + BEST-PRACTICES.md in the project root contain dense lessons-learned content (22 pitfalls, model-selection cheat-sheet, prompt-engineering meta, locked prompt templates) that has been extracted into TEMPLATE.md / model-stack.md / prompt-cookbook.md. Future projects using this template should run /postmortem to produce the canonical 6-file split and feed it back.
